Source: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks (Lewis et al.)
Method/Approach: RAG architecture combining dense retrieval with seq2seq models
KeyFindings: RAG outperforms state-of-art on open-domain QA, achieving 44.5% exact match on Natural Questions dataset
Limitations: Requires large-scale pre-training, high computational cost, no confidence scoring mechanism
IdentifiedGap: Lack of confidence metrics for generated responses; no multi-tenancy support
Relevance: Foundational RAG architecture; demonstrates effectiveness of retrieval-augmented generation for knowledge tasks

Source: Enterprise Knowledge Management Systems: A Review (Alavi & Leidner)
Method/Approach: Literature review of KM systems and organizational knowledge processes
KeyFindings: Knowledge codification vs personalization strategies; technology enablers include repositories, directories, and networks
Limitations: Focus on traditional KM without AI/ML; pre-dates semantic search and embeddings
IdentifiedGap: Gap between knowledge storage and efficient retrieval; no natural language interfaces
Relevance: Establishes theoretical foundation for organizational knowledge problems; highlights retrieval efficiency challenges

Source: FAISS: A Library for Efficient Similarity Search (Johnson et al.)
Method/Approach: Vector indexing algorithms for billion-scale similarity search
KeyFindings: IndexFlatIP provides exact cosine similarity; IndexIVFFlat offers speed-accuracy tradeoffs; GPU acceleration achieves 8.5x speedup
Limitations: Exact search (Flat indices) slower for >10M vectors; approximate methods trade accuracy for speed
IdentifiedGap: Multi-tenant vector isolation not addressed; no organization-scoped index management
Relevance: Provides technical foundation for vector search; demonstrates scalability for production RAG systems

Source: Multi-Tenancy in Cloud Applications: A Survey (Bezemer & Zaidman)
Method/Approach: Taxonomy of multi-tenancy patterns and isolation mechanisms
KeyFindings: Shared-database with row-level filtering most cost-effective; complete isolation most secure but expensive
Limitations: Performance overhead of RLS policies not quantified for ML workloads; no RAG-specific guidance
IdentifiedGap: Lack of best practices for multi-tenant vector databases and embedding isolation
Relevance: Establishes multi-tenancy design patterns; highlights need for data isolation in SaaS applications

Source: Confidence Estimation for Machine Translation (Ueffing & Ney)
Method/Approach: Confidence scoring methods for seq2seq models
KeyFindings: Word-level and sentence-level confidence metrics improve translation quality assessment
Limitations: Domain-specific to machine translation; not generalized to RAG or knowledge retrieval
IdentifiedGap: Multi-factor confidence scoring for RAG responses; citation-aware confidence metrics
Relevance: Demonstrates feasibility of confidence scoring for generated text; provides methodological foundation

Source: Customer Support Ticket Routing Using Deep Learning (Jain et al.)
KeyFindings: Deep learning reduces misrouting by 23%; automated assignment improves first-response time by 18%
Limitations: No knowledge retrieval component; routing-only solution; requires large labeled datasets
IdentifiedGap: Integration of ticket routing with AI-powered knowledge assistance; RAG for support workflows
Relevance: Shows AI effectiveness in support operations; demonstrates ROI potential for ML in customer service
